title: AuditLogEvent
description: Schema for a compliant audit log event. Every field must be populated at write time. Events are immutable once written.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this audit event. Use UUID v4. Enables deduplication.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of the event in ISO 8601 format with timezone designator. Example: 2026-04-05T14:32:00.123Z

actor.description: The entity that performed the action.

id.description: Stable identifier for the actor. For users: the user UUID. For services: the service name and instance ID.

type.description: Category of actor. 'user' = human authenticated via UI/API. 'service' = machine-to-machine. 'system' = automated background task.

email.description: Email address of the human actor (users only). Null for service accounts.

roles.description: Roles held by the actor at the time of the action. Snapshot — not a live lookup.

impersonated_by.description: If this action was performed via impersonation, the ID of the user who initiated the impersonation. Null otherwise.

action.description: Verb describing what was done. Use a consistent vocabulary: create, read, update, delete, list, login, logout, export, import, approve, reject, execute, cancel.

resource.description: The object that was acted upon.

type.description: Resource type in snake_case. Examples: experiment, user, api_key, config, report.

id.description: Identifier of the specific resource instance. Use the resource's primary key.

name.description: Human-readable name of the resource at the time of the action. Snapshot — the resource name may change later.

parent_id.description: If the resource is nested (e.g. an action within an experiment), the parent resource ID.

parent_type.description: Type of the parent resource, if applicable.

outcome.description: Whether the action succeeded or failed. Always log both outcomes — failed attempts are often more significant than successful ones.

failure_reason.description: For failed outcomes: a machine-readable reason code. Examples: not_found, forbidden, validation_error, rate_limited. Do not include sensitive data.

ip_address.description: Source IP address of the request. May be null for internal system events with no network origin.

user_agent.description: HTTP User-Agent header value, if available. Null for service-to-service calls.

correlation_id.description: Distributed trace / request ID that ties this event to a specific request or workflow. Use the X-Request-ID or trace ID from OpenTelemetry.

session_id.description: Session identifier, if applicable. Enables grouping of actions within a single user session.

service.description: The service that generated this audit event.

name.description: Service name as deployed. Examples: chaos-platform-api, experiment-runner.

metadata.description: Optional key-value pairs for additional context specific to the action. Must not contain PII or secrets. Keys must be snake_case.
